data.title: Debate Over War Powers Act Deadline for Military Action
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump faces scrutiny over the 60-day deadline set by the 1973 War Powers Act for military actions without congressional approval. According to Odaily, some lawmakers argue that May 1 marks the deadline, as Trump notified Congress of hostilities on March 2. However, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claims the current ceasefire pauses the 60-day countdown.

Senator Adam Schiff disagrees, stating that the ceasefire does not halt the clock and argues the military action was illegal from the start due to the absence of an imminent threat. Meanwhile, Republican Senator Lisa Murkowski warns that if the White House fails to present a credible plan next week, a proposal for an Authorization for Use of Military Force (AUMF) will be introduced to uphold Congress's constitutional oversight role. It remains uncertain whether Trump will seek an additional 30-day extension allowed by the act.

data.title: Sky Enhances Governance with Laniakea Infrastructure Targeting Idle Stablecoins
data.description: Sky has announced the launch of its institutional-grade infrastructure, Laniakea, as part of its governance reform efforts. According to PANews, this initiative aims to tap into the $300 billion worth of idle stablecoins, intensifying competition in the DeFi infrastructure sector. The move is seen as a strategic step to leverage the vast amount of unused stablecoins in the decentralized finance space, potentially reshaping the landscape of DeFi infrastructure.

data.title: Senators including Warren questioned Tether and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick about related-party lending issues.
data.description: U.S. Democratic Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden sent letters this week to Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino and U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, expressing concerns about Tether's loan to a family trust fund with Lutnick's children as beneficiaries. The letter notes that Lutnick previously led Cantor Fitzgerald, which has served as custodian of Tether's reserves since 2021. The senators questioned whether the loan could have assisted Lutnick's children in obtaining funds to acquire their father's shares in Cantor Fitzgerald, and expressed concern that this involved bribery or other improper conduct. Furthermore, the senators expressed concerns about Tether's past compliance record and its lobbying efforts during the GENIUS Act legislation process, emphasizing the need to ensure that politically connected crypto stakeholders do not receive preferential treatment. (The Block)

data.title: A suspected Multicoin address holds $26.9 million in HYPE, with a paper profit of approximately $10.5 million.
data.description: Arkham posted on the X platform that Arkham AI discovered a suspected Multicoin address. This address previously received $39.4 million from Galaxy Digital OTC and then deposited $22.99 million into Coinbase. Currently, the address still holds $26.9 million in HYPE, with a floating profit of approximately $10.5 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs See Inflows as Ethereum Continues Outflows
data.description: Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced a net inflow of $14.7578 million on April 30 (ET), according to NS3.AI. This marks the end of three consecutive days of net outflows for Bitcoin. In contrast, Ethereum spot ETFs recorded a net outflow of $23.6426 million, continuing a four-day trend of outflows.

data.title: The US Congress is divided on whether a war with Iran would trigger the 60-day deadline of the War Powers Act.
data.description: Under the War Powers Act of 1973, the president must cease using force after a 60-day period if he initiates military action without congressional authorization. Currently, some lawmakers believe May 1st is the deadline, arguing that Trump informed Congress of the start of hostilities on March 2nd. However, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that the current ceasefire means the 60-day clock has been paused or stopped. Senator Adam Schiff countered that a ceasefire does not stop the clock and argued that the military action was illegal from the outset because there was no imminent threat. Meanwhile, some Republican lawmakers, such as Lisa Murkowski, indicated they would introduce an Authorized Use of Force (AUMF) proposal to fulfill Congress's constitutional oversight responsibility if the White House fails to present a credible plan by next week. Currently, it remains uncertain whether Trump will request an additional 30-day extension allowed by the bill. (CNN)

data.title: Hundreds of Wallets Emptied on Ethereum Mainnet in Suspected Real-Time Vulnerability Attack
data.description: According to Odaily, anonymous blockchain investigator Wazz has revealed that hundreds of wallets on the Ethereum mainnet have been emptied by a single address. Several of these wallets had been inactive for over seven years. The incident is suspected to be a new type of real-time vulnerability attack. Crypto user Capitulation commented that the most likely cause of the vulnerability was storing mnemonic phrases in LastPass's secure notes during 2020/21.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $23.6426 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $23.6426 million yesterday (April 30th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock's Stashed ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $29.1038 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $70.8207 million. This was followed by Grayscale's Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $4.7213 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.93 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock's ETH ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $50.5739 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $143 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $13.253 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.85%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.915 billion.

data.title: Singapore PM Warns of Global Disruptions Amid AI Rise and Iran Conflict
data.description: Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong has issued a warning about potential disruptions as the nation faces an increasingly uncertain global landscape. Bloomberg posted on X that Wong highlighted the Iran conflict and the swift advancement of artificial intelligence as key factors contributing to this uncertainty. He emphasized the need for Singapore to remain vigilant and adaptable in response to these challenges. Wong's remarks underscore the importance of strategic planning and resilience in navigating the complexities of the current global environment.

data.title: Hundreds of Ethereum mainnet wallets are suspected of being attacked by the same address, some of which have been inactive for over 7 years, possibly related to LastPass security notes.
data.description: According to anonymous blockchain investigator Wazz, hundreds of wallets were emptied from the same address on the Ethereum mainnet, many of which had been inactive for over seven years. This incident is suspected to be a new type of real-time vulnerability attack. Crypto user Capitulation commented that the most likely vulnerability stemmed from placing mnemonic phrases in LastPass's security notes during 2020/21.

data.title: Bitcoin Short-Term Holders Show Positive Trend in SOPR
data.description:
Bitcoin's short-term holders are experiencing a positive shift in their SOPR (Spent Output Profit Ratio), according to ChainCatcher. Crypto analyst Darkfost notes that the 30-day moving average of the SOPR for short-term holders has turned positive for the first time in six months. This indicates that these holders are either breaking even or seeing slight profits.

Historically, such trends have acted as resistance levels during bear markets, often leading to sell-offs due to lack of confidence among holders.

data.title: Emerging-Market Asian Dollar Bonds See Unprecedented Yield Premiums
data.description: Yield premiums on emerging-market Asian investment-grade dollar bonds have tightened to unprecedented levels. Bloomberg posted on X that this development comes amid reduced new issuance and China's economic resilience, despite disruptions caused by the Iran conflict. The tightening of yield premiums reflects investor confidence in the region's economic stability, even as geopolitical tensions persist. Analysts suggest that the limited supply of new bonds has contributed to the narrowing of yield spreads, highlighting the attractiveness of existing bonds in the market. China's ability to maintain economic growth despite external challenges has further bolstered investor sentiment, leading to increased demand for Asian dollar bonds. This trend underscores the region's potential to weather global uncertainties and maintain financial stability.

data.title: Stable Token Scheduled for Major Unlock on May 8
data.description: Stable (STABLE) is set to unlock approximately 888.89 million tokens on May 8 at 13:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is valued at around $29.81 million. The event is part of the token's planned distribution schedule.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 620 USDT with a 0.47% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 01, 2026, 03:20 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 620 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 620.390015 USDT, with a narrowed 0.47% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 77,000 USDT with a 1.96% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 01, 2026, 03:20 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 77,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 77,264.789063 USDT, with a narrowed 1.96% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Expert Predicts RWA Infrastructure to Transform Financial Markets by 2030
data.description: Wish Wu has projected that the development of Real-World Asset (RWA) infrastructure could drive financial markets towards near real-time settlement and expanded global access by 2030. According to NS3.AI, Wu emphasized that stablecoins and advanced blockchain infrastructure might facilitate automated execution, integrated compliance, and the creation of more programmable financial products.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Bank of America Revises Aluminum and Zinc Price Forecasts Amid Middle East Supply Disruptions
data.description: Bank of America has adjusted its aluminum price target, moving the forecast of $4,000 per ton from the second quarter of 2027 to the fourth quarter of 2026 due to damage to smelters in the Middle East. According to Jin10, analyst Michael Widmer from the bank also raised the long-term price expectations for aluminum. Additionally, Bank of America increased its average zinc price forecast for 2026 by 13% to $3,309 per ton, citing tight market supply. Widmer noted that adjustments for other metals were minor. He added that while the Middle East conflict might impact both metal demand and supply, the demand shock is not significant enough to alter the current baseline supply-demand balance.

data.title: Cathie Wood Reflects on ARK Invest's Early Bitcoin Adoption
data.description: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, shared insights on the firm's initial investment in Bitcoin back in 2015. According to NS3.AI, Wood mentioned that ARK Invest faced widespread skepticism when it first took a position in Bitcoin. Despite the doubts, this skepticism fueled her interest in the technology. Wood now views Bitcoin as a representation of a global monetary system.

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Rise Amid Market Volatility
data.description: WTI crude oil prices increased by 0.55%, reaching $108.44 per barrel, while Brent crude saw a 0.65% rise to $117.80, according to NS3.AI. The market snapshot also indicated gold trading at $4,629.57 per ounce and silver at $74.404. Meanwhile, the volatility indexes for Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) experienced declines, reflecting a shift in market dynamics.

data.title: The X platform's Cashtags feature is now available on the web.
data.description: Nikita Bier, the product manager for X, stated in an article on the X platform that the Cashtags feature has been launched on the web, and X will become a core component of the user's trading terminal, providing real-time charts and posts for each asset.
